levels.title: Level 1 - Foundation (1st Year)
levels.description: Master the rock-solid basics of programming logic, core data structures, and the foundational front-end web stack.

modules.title: Module 1 - Computer & Programming Basics
modules.description: How computers work, logic, and algorithms basics.

content.explanation: A computer consists of hardware (physical components like CPU, RAM) and software (instructions that tell the hardware what to do).
content.instructions: Read up on the CPU cycle (Fetch, Decode, Execute). Understand the difference between volatile memory (RAM) and non-volatile storage.
content.example: Hardware = The kitchen; Software = The recipe.

content.explanation: The internet is a global network of computers connected via wires. Data is sent in packets using protocols like HTTP and TCP/IP.
content.instructions: Learn the concepts of IP Addresses, Domain Name System (DNS), and the Client-Server model.
content.example: Client (Your browser) requests 'google.com' -> DNS maps it to an IP -> Server responds with HTML.

content.explanation: Pseudocode is a plain language description of the steps in an algorithm. It ignores syntax and focuses purely on logic.
content.instructions: Write pseudocode for checking if a student has passed an exam (Pass mark = 40).
content.example:
IF marks >= 40 THEN
  PRINT 'Pass'
ELSE
  PRINT 'Fail'
ENDIF

content.explanation: Flowcharts use shapes to represent steps: Ovals for Start/End, Parallelograms for Input/Output, Rectangles for Process, Diamonds for Decisions.
content.instructions: Draw a flowchart for finding the largest of three numbers.
content.example: Start -> Input A,B,C -> Is A > B? -> (Yes) Is A > C? -> Output A

content.explanation: Creating a real-world algorithm using loops and conditions.
content.instructions: Write a complete step-by-step algorithm for an ATM system. Include PIN verification (max 3 tries), balance check, and withdrawal limits.
content.example:
1. Initialize attempts = 0
2. Prompt for PIN
3. IF PIN is correct, show menu
4. ELSE increment attempts...

modules.title: Module 2 - Programming Fundamentals
modules.description: Variables, data types, conditions, loops, and functions.

content.explanation: Variables store data. Data types include integers, floats, characters, strings, and booleans. Operators allow math and comparison.
content.instructions: Understand declaration, initialization, and arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /, %).

content.explanation: Conditions allow code to branch based on decisions.
content.instructions: Learn how if, else if, else, and switch-case statements work.
content.example: if (score >= 90) { grade = 'A'; }

content.explanation: Loops execute a block of code multiple times. Core loops are For, While, and Do-While.
content.instructions: Learn the syntax for iterating arrays and repeating actions until a condition is met.

content.explanation: Pattern problems build strong nested loop logic.
content.instructions: Write code to print a right-angled triangle and a pyramid using stars (*).

content.explanation: Functions are reusable blocks of code. Scope defines where variables can be accessed.
content.instructions: Create functions with parameters and return values. Understand global vs local scope.
content.example: function add(a, b) { return a + b; }

content.explanation: Combine logic, random number generation, and loops.
content.instructions: Build a game where the computer picks a random number from 1-100. Let the user guess, and provide 'Too high' or 'Too low' hints until they win.
content.example: while(guess !== target) { ... }

modules.title: Module 3 - Web Development Basics
modules.description: HTML, CSS, Flexbox, Responsive basics, JavaScript basics.

content.explanation: HTML forms the skeleton of a web page using semantic tags.
content.instructions: Learn how to use <header>, <nav>, <main>, <section>, and <footer> correctly.
content.example:
<main>
  <section id='hero'>...</section>
</main>

content.explanation: CSS makes things look beautiful. Selectors target HTML elements.
content.instructions: Learn class selectors (.class), ID selectors (#id), colors, margins, padding, and fonts.
content.example: .card { padding: 20px; background: #fff; }

content.explanation: Flexbox is a 1D layout model for aligning items in rows or columns.
content.instructions: Create a responsive navigation bar and a 3-column feature grid using display: flex.
content.example: display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;

content.explanation: JavaScript interacts with HTML through the Document Object Model (DOM).
content.instructions: Learn querySelector, addEventListener, and how to change textContent and classes dynamically.

content.explanation: Build your own personal space on the internet.
content.instructions: Create a static portfolio page containing a Hero Section, About Me, Skills Grid (using Flexbox), and a Contact Form UI.
content.example: HTML

modules.title: Module 4 - Problem Solving
modules.description: Logic building, arrays, strings, and the dry run method.

content.explanation: Dry running means manually tracing variables on paper step-by-step.
content.instructions: Write down a simple loop and create a table tracking the value of 'i' and the output at every iteration.
content.example:
Iteration 1: i=0, sum=5
Iteration 2: i=1, sum=10

content.explanation: Iterating through arrays to find answers.
content.instructions: Solve three problems: 1. Find the sum of an array. 2. Find the maximum element. 3. Count even numbers in an array.

content.explanation: Strings are arrays of characters.
content.instructions: Write algorithms to count vowels in a string, and reverse a string manually without built-in methods.

content.explanation: A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards (e.g., 'racecar').
content.instructions: Implement an algorithm using the two-pointer approach to check if a string is a palindrome. Output true or false.
content.example:
let left = 0, right = str.length - 1;
while(left < right) { ... }

modules.title: Module 5 - Git & GitHub
modules.description: Git basics, GitHub basics, push/pull, repository creation.

content.explanation: Version control lets you track changes to files over time, revert to old versions, and collaborate.
content.instructions: Understand the difference between Git (the tool) and GitHub (the hosting service).
content.example: Git is like taking photos of your code. GitHub is like an album online.

content.explanation: The core workflow of saving local changes.
content.instructions: Initialize a repository in a test folder. Create a text file, add it to staging area, and commit it with a clear message.
content.example:
git init
git add .
git commit -m 'Initial commit'

content.explanation: Syncing local code with a remote server.
content.instructions: Learn how git remote add, git push, and git pull work to keep branches up to date.
content.example: git push -u origin main

content.explanation: Host your code online for the world to see.
content.instructions: Create a GitHub account. Create a new repository. Link your local Portfolio UI folder to this repo, commit all code, and push it. Enable GitHub Pages to host it live.
content.example: Go to Settings -> Pages -> Deploy from main branch.

modules.title: Module 6 - Communication & Soft Skills
modules.description: English communication, confidence building, LinkedIn basics, resume basics.

content.explanation: Clear and polite communication sets you apart.
content.instructions: Learn the structure of a professional email: Subject Line, Salutation, Body (brief & direct), Sign-off.
content.example:
Subject: Request for Internship Opportunity
Dear [Name],
...

content.explanation: An elevator pitch is a 30-second introduction summarizing who you are and what you do.
content.instructions: Write down a 4-sentence introduction about yourself, your skills, and your current learning focus. Practice speaking it aloud.
content.example: Hi, I'm Niran, a first-year CSE student passionate about frontend web development...

content.explanation: A resume is a 1-page summary of your professional value.
content.instructions: Learn what sections a tech resume needs: Contact, Education, Skills, Projects. (Experience can be left blank or filled with extracurriculars for now).
content.example: Use Action Verbs: 'Developed', 'Designed', 'Implemented'.

content.explanation: LinkedIn is your online, interactive resume and networking hub.
content.instructions: Create a LinkedIn profile. Add a professional photo, write a headline, put your elevator pitch in the 'About' section, and add your college education.
content.example: Headline: First Year Computer Science Student | Aspiring Web Developer

modules.title: Module 7 - Aptitude Foundation
modules.description: Percentages, ratios, number systems, logical reasoning.

content.explanation: Basic mathematical intuition is heavily tested in campus placements.
content.instructions: Review prime numbers, LCM, HCF, and fraction-to-percentage conversions (e.g., 1/8 = 12.5%).
content.example: Memorize tables up to 20 and squares up to 30.

content.explanation: Solving speed problems.
content.instructions: Solve 5 percentage increase/decrease problems and 5 ratio sharing problems.
content.example: If A's salary is 20% more than B's, by what % is B's salary less than A's?

content.explanation: Identifying patterns in series and shapes.
content.instructions: Learn how to approach number series (difference, multiplication, squares) and blood relation puzzles.
content.example: Find the next: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30... (Answer: 42)

content.explanation: Test your speed and accuracy.
content.instructions: Take a 30-minute online mock test covering basic maths and logic. Aim for 80% accuracy.
content.example: Search for 'TCS NQT basic mock test' online and attempt it.

modules.title: Module 8 - Mini Project Zone
modules.description: Build real projects using HTML, CSS, and JS.

content.explanation: Combine CSS Grid for UI and JS logic for evaluation.
content.instructions: Create a calculator that can add, subtract, multiply, and divide. Handle edge cases like dividing by zero.
content.example: Use eval() cautiously, or build a custom parser for safety.

content.explanation: Learn CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) locally.
content.instructions: Build an app where you can add tasks, mark them complete (strikethrough), and delete them. Save tasks to localStorage so they persist on reload.
content.example: localStorage.setItem('tasks', JSON.stringify(taskList));

content.explanation: Handling arrays of objects and dynamic HTML generation.
content.instructions: Create a dashboard that takes an array of student objects (name, marks) and renders a table. Highlight students who failed in red.
content.example: students.map(s => `${s.name}...`)

content.explanation: Working with asynchronous JavaScript and external REST APIs.
content.instructions: Use the OpenWeatherMap API (or similar free API). Build an interface with a search bar that fetches and displays the current temperature, humidity, and weather icon for a given city.
content.example: const res = await fetch(`https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q=${city}&appid=${API_KEY}`);

modules.title: Module 9 - Java & Object Oriented Programming
modules.description: Learn Java fundamentals, classes, objects, inheritance, and polymorphism.

content.explanation: Java is a statically typed, object-oriented language. Understand JVM, JDK, and JRE.
content.instructions: Write your first 'Hello World' in Java and understand the public static void main syntax.
content.example: public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) { System.out.println("Hello World"); } }

content.explanation: Classes are blueprints, and objects are instances of those blueprints.
content.instructions: Create a 'Car' class with attributes (color, brand) and methods (drive, brake). Instantiate two objects.
content.example: Car myCar = new Car(); myCar.color = "Red";

content.explanation: Inheritance allows a class to inherit properties from another. Polymorphism allows methods to do different things based on the object.
content.instructions: Create a base class 'Animal' and subclasses 'Dog' and 'Cat' that override the 'makeSound' method.
content.example: class Dog extends Animal { @Override void makeSound() { System.out.println("Bark"); } }

content.explanation: Apply OOP concepts to build a small application.
content.instructions: Build a command-line Library Management System using Java where users can add books, borrow books, and return books using classes and lists.
content.example: List<Book> library = new ArrayList<>();

modules.title: Module 10 - Data Structures & Algorithms
modules.description: Introduction to fundamental Data Structures like Stacks, Queues, Linked Lists and basic Algorithms.

content.explanation: Big O notation describes the performance of an algorithm.
content.instructions: Understand O(1), O(n), O(n^2), and O(log n). Analyze the complexity of a simple loop vs nested loops.
content.example: A single loop from 1 to N is O(N).

content.explanation: A linked list is a linear data structure where elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations.
content.instructions: Implement a Singly Linked List in Java or JS with insertAtEnd and printList methods.
content.example: class Node { int data; Node next; }

content.explanation: Stack follows LIFO (Last In First Out). Queue follows FIFO (First In First Out).
content.instructions: Implement a Stack using an Array. Then, use the stack to solve the 'Valid Parentheses' problem.
content.example: stack.push(1); int top = stack.pop();

content.explanation: Sorting rearranges elements in a specific order.
content.instructions: Implement Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Insertion Sort. Compare their execution times on a large array.
content.example: for(int i=0; i<n-1; i++) { if(arr[j] > arr[j+1]) swap(arr[j], arr[j+1]); }

levels.title: Level 2 - Core Skills (2nd Year)
levels.description: Deep dive into web frameworks and architecture.

content.explanation: Coming Soon

levels.title: Level 3 - Advanced Topics (3rd Year)
levels.description: System design, advanced DSA, and complex architectures.

content.explanation: Coming Soon
